mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines below 5.5% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Either GDPC1 or HOUST shows negative growth in 3-month trailing window, or 60-day forward returns in combined growth scenarios fall below 0.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.0 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that improves corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or INDPRO fails to sustain above 102.5 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, increasing real interest rates and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or CPIAUCSL declines below 2.0% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust job market expansion that increases consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS falls below 100k without corresponding equity declines.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Either RSAFS or HOUST shows negative growth in 3-month trailing window, or 20-day forward returns in combined growth scenarios fall below 0.3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly federal funds rate increase above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or FEDFUNDS declines below 1.5% without corresponding equity gains.
